description: To customize the style of any result layer, click on the Style icon next to the layer name.
In the style editor, choose a sub-category and click on its Fill or Stroke color box.
Select a color from the palette, adjust transparency using the slider, and click Apply, followed by Submit to save the changes.
Your map will now reflect the updated visual styling for easier interpretation.
To divide a result layer or AOI into grids, click on the Grid icon beside the layer name.
Use the ‘+’ or ‘–’ buttons in the pop-up window to set the desired grid count (e.g., 6x6, 10x10).
Click Submit to generate the grid overlay on the map.
You can now click on individual grid tiles to view localized statistics and analytics — perfect for area-specific resource planning and task assignment.
To visualize data as a Hotspot map, select the Hotspot option from the layer menu.
The map will immediately display the hotspot visualization.
You can adjust the blur and radius sliders to fine-tune the visualization according to your preference.
